libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: No environment or system-property setup is required. Generated tests should keep coverage in-process: create a temporary webapp directory with WEB-INF/web.xml/static content, start an embedded Jetty Server/WebAppContext on an ephemeral loopback port, and shut it down in finally/cleanup. For the compile failure specifically, note that WebAppClassLoader.Context changed in 9.4.0.RC1: isSystemClass/isServerClass take Class<?> and the interface also requires isServerResource(String, URL) and isSystemResource(String, URL).

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8856 [Automation] javac compile fails for org.eclipse.jetty:jetty-webapp:9.4.0.RC1; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015

risks: Avoid adding JSP/JSTL coverage unless explicitly adding the separate Jetty JSP artifacts; basic jetty-webapp coverage does not require them.
The existing failure is an API-compatibility change in test code, not missing external setup.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: No extra dependency, Docker image, environment variable, or system property is needed. jetty-webapp 9.4.0.RC1 resolves its normal embedded-webapp stack transitively, including jetty-servlet, jetty-security, jetty-server, jetty-http, jetty-io, jetty-util, jetty-xml, and javax.servlet-api 3.1.0; meaningful coverage is reachable with an in-process WebAppContext and temporary WEB-INF/web.xml.
